speaker: WIDOW
text: Nay, come, for if they do approach the city, we

speaker: WIDOW
text: shall lose all the sight.

speaker: DIANA
text: They say the French count has done most honorable

speaker: DIANA
text: service.

speaker: WIDOW
text: It is reported that he has taken their great’st

speaker: WIDOW
text: commander, and that with his own hand he slew

speaker: WIDOW
text:
the Duke’s brother. 
                A
                 
                trumpet
                 
                sounds
                .
               We have

speaker: WIDOW
text: lost our labor. They are gone a contrary way. Hark,

speaker: WIDOW
text: you may know by their trumpets.

speaker: MARIANA
text: Come, let’s return again and suffice ourselves

speaker: MARIANA
text: with the report of it.—Well, Diana, take heed of

speaker: MARIANA
text: this French earl. The honor of a maid is her name,

speaker: MARIANA
text: and no legacy is so rich as honesty.

speaker: WIDOW
text: I have told my neighbor how you

speaker: WIDOW
text: have been solicited by a gentleman, his

speaker: WIDOW
text: companion.

speaker: MARIANA
text: I know that knave, hang him! One Parolles, a

speaker: MARIANA
text: filthy officer he is in those suggestions for the

speaker: MARIANA
text: young earl.—Beware of them, Diana. Their promises,

speaker: MARIANA
text: enticements, oaths, tokens, and all these

speaker: MARIANA
text: engines of lust are not the things they go under.

speaker: MARIANA
text: Many a maid hath been seduced by them; and

speaker: MARIANA
text: the misery is example that so terrible shows in the

speaker: MARIANA
text: wrack of maidenhood cannot for all that dissuade

speaker: MARIANA
text: succession, but that they are limed with the twigs

speaker: MARIANA
text: that threatens them. I hope I need not to advise

speaker: MARIANA
text: you further, but I hope your own grace will keep

speaker: MARIANA
text: you where you are, though there were no further

speaker: MARIANA
text: danger known but the modesty which is so lost.

speaker: DIANA
text: You shall not need to fear me.

speaker: WIDOW
text: I hope so.

speaker: WIDOW
text: Look, here comes a pilgrim. I know she will lie at

speaker: WIDOW
text: my house; thither they send one another. I’ll question

speaker: WIDOW
text: her.—God save you, pilgrim. Whither are

speaker: WIDOW
text: bound?

speaker: HELEN
text: To Saint Jaques le Grand.

speaker: HELEN
text: Where do the palmers lodge, I do beseech you?

speaker: WIDOW
text: At the Saint Francis here beside the port.

speaker: HELEN
text: Is this the way?

speaker: WIDOW
text: Ay, marry, is ’t.—Hark you, they come this way.—

speaker: WIDOW
text: If you will tarry, holy pilgrim,

speaker: WIDOW
text: But till the troops come by,

speaker: WIDOW
text: I will conduct you where you shall be lodged,

speaker: WIDOW
text: The rather for I think I know your hostess

speaker: WIDOW
text: As ample as myself.

speaker: HELEN
text: Is it yourself?

speaker: WIDOW
text: If you shall please so, pilgrim.

speaker: HELEN
text: I thank you, and will stay upon your leisure.

speaker: WIDOW
text: You came I think from France?

speaker: HELEN
text: I did so.

speaker: WIDOW
text: Here you shall see a countryman of yours

speaker: WIDOW
text: That has done worthy service.

speaker: HELEN
text: His name, I pray you?

speaker: DIANA
text: The Count Rossillion. Know you such a one?

speaker: HELEN
text: But by the ear, that hears most nobly of him.

speaker: HELEN
text: His face I know not.

speaker: DIANA
text: Whatsome’er he is,

speaker: DIANA
text: He’s bravely taken here. He stole from France,

speaker: DIANA
text: As ’tis reported, for the King had married him

speaker: DIANA
text: Against his liking. Think you it is so?

speaker: HELEN
text: Ay, surely, mere the truth. I know his lady.

speaker: DIANA
text: There is a gentleman that serves the Count

speaker: DIANA
text: Reports but coarsely of her.

speaker: HELEN
text: What’s his name?

speaker: DIANA
text: Monsieur Parolles.

speaker: HELEN
text: O, I believe with him.

speaker: HELEN
text: In argument of praise, or to the worth

speaker: HELEN
text: Of the great count himself, she is too mean

speaker: HELEN
text: To have her name repeated. All her deserving

speaker: HELEN
text: Is a reservèd honesty, and that

speaker: HELEN
text: I have not heard examined.

speaker: DIANA
text: Alas, poor lady,

speaker: DIANA
text: ’Tis a hard bondage to become the wife

speaker: DIANA
text: Of a detesting lord.

speaker: WIDOW
text: I warrant, good creature, wheresoe’er she is,

speaker: WIDOW
text: Her heart weighs sadly. This young maid might do her

speaker: WIDOW
text: A shrewd turn if she pleased.

speaker: HELEN
text: How do you mean?

speaker: HELEN
text: Maybe the amorous count solicits her

speaker: HELEN
text: In the unlawful purpose?

speaker: WIDOW
text: He does indeed,

speaker: WIDOW
text: And brokes with all that can in such a suit

speaker: WIDOW
text: Corrupt the tender honor of a maid,

speaker: WIDOW
text: But she is armed for him and keeps her guard

speaker: WIDOW
text: In honestest defense.

speaker: MARIANA
text: The gods forbid else!

speaker: WIDOW
text: So, now they come.

speaker: WIDOW
text: That is Antonio, the Duke’s eldest son;

speaker: WIDOW
text: That, Escalus.

speaker: HELEN
text: Which is the Frenchman?

speaker: DIANA
text: He,

speaker: DIANA
text: That with the plume. ’Tis a most gallant fellow.

speaker: DIANA
text: I would he loved his wife. If he were honester,

speaker: DIANA
text: He were much goodlier. Is ’t not a handsome gentleman?

speaker: HELEN
text: I like him well.

speaker: DIANA
text: ’Tis pity he is not honest. Yond’s that same knave

speaker: DIANA
text: That leads him to these places. Were I his lady,

speaker: DIANA
text: I would poison that vile rascal.

speaker: HELEN
text: Which is he?

speaker: DIANA
text: That jackanapes with scarves. Why is he melancholy?

speaker: HELEN
text: Perchance he’s hurt i’ th’ battle.

speaker: PAROLLES
text: Lose our drum? Well.

speaker: MARIANA
text: He’s shrewdly vexed at something. Look, he

speaker: MARIANA
text: has spied us.

speaker: WIDOW
text: Marry, hang you.

speaker: MARIANA
text: And your courtesy, for a

speaker: MARIANA
text: ring-carrier.

speaker: WIDOW
text: The troop is passed. Come, pilgrim, I will bring you

speaker: WIDOW
text: Where you shall host. Of enjoined penitents

speaker: WIDOW
text: There’s four or five, to Great Saint Jaques bound,

speaker: WIDOW
text: Already at my house.

speaker: HELEN
text: I humbly thank you.

speaker: HELEN
text: Please it this matron and this gentle maid

speaker: HELEN
text: To eat with us tonight, the charge and thanking

speaker: HELEN
text: Shall be for me. And to requite you further,

speaker: HELEN
text: I will bestow some precepts of this virgin

speaker: HELEN
text: Worthy the note.

speaker: BOTH
text: We’ll take your offer kindly.
